steps.title: Pin down the assumption being challenged
steps.content: The underlined assumption is specific: domed nests $\Rightarrow$ larger geographic ranges than open nests. A finding that challenges this must show the opposite pattern — open-nest species having ranges as large as or larger than domed-nest species.

steps.title: Eliminate findings that don't address geographic range
steps.content:
• "Species that build open nests tend to have higher extinction rates than species that build domed nests.": Compares extinction rates, not geographic ranges — irrelevant to the assumption. ✗
  • "Species that build open nests tend to be smaller in size than species that build domed nests.": Compares body size, not geographic ranges — irrelevant to the assumption. ✗
  • "Species that build open nests tend to use fewer materials to build their nests than species that build domed nests do.": Compares nest materials, not geographic ranges — irrelevant to the assumption. ✗
  • "Species that build open nests tend to have larger ranges than species that build domed nests.": Directly contradicts the assumption that domed-nest species have larger ranges, challenging it head-on. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
